questions.explanation: A moving charge produces both electric field (due to charge) and magnetic field (due to motion).

questions.text: The force on a charged particle moving in a magnetic field is given by F = qvB sin θ.
questions.explanation: The magnetic force on a moving charge is F = qvB sin θ, where θ is the angle between velocity and magnetic field.

questions.text: The SI unit of magnetic field is ________.
questions.explanation: The SI unit of magnetic field B is Tesla (T). 1 T = 1 N/(A·m)

questions.correctAnswer: If you hold a current-carrying conductor in your right hand with thumb pointing in direction of current, fingers curl in direction of magnetic field.
questions.explanation: Right-hand thumb rule gives the direction of magnetic field around a current-carrying conductor.
